title: phoradendron kingii
intro: Phoradendron kingii, also known as King's Manzanita or King's Mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of certain trees, specifically the Arizona Cypress and the Coulter Pine, in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Phoradendron kingii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by boiling the leaves in water or extracting the plant's compounds with a solvent.
